notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Tenant migration to the EU sovereign region
summary: The tenant migrates to a new region and residency; review the new boundary before publishing.

affected_scope.scope_summary: Managed-cloud tenant migrating from US-east to the EU sovereign region.

axes.summary: Tenant identity moved to the EU tenant.
Residency moved to the EU sovereign scope.

boundary_change.summary: Tenant, region, and residency changed; review before the next publish.

blocked_writes.note: Policy admin writes wait for a fresh approval under the new tenant.
Provider publish is held as a local draft until the new boundary is reviewed.

succeeded_hosted_mutations.note: Settings sync replicated to the new tenant before cutover.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local search continue.
Local export of the workspace continues.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local work continues; managed publishes wait for the new-boundary review.
